title: I noticed there were some changes in the

messages.author: Matlavox
messages.text: yeah, I guess it's just like, how do I know for sure it worked? Like what if the device was unknowingly off, and I press PWR for 1.5 seconds thinking I'm turning it off, but unknowingly turning it on? There's no power up LED or anything
messages.content_html: yeah, I guess it's just like, how do I know for sure it worked? Like what if the device was unknowingly off, and I press PWR for 1.5 seconds thinking I'm turning it off, but unknowingly turning it on? There's no power up LED or anything
messages.reply_html:
Christopher A. Wichura
Looking at https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/1873, the power off hold time is defined as 1.5 seconds.

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: All I see is it explicitly turning the LEDs off when shutting down. Does it not turn the green LED on when it boots?
messages.content_html: All I see is it explicitly turning the LEDs off when shutting down. Does it not turn the green LED on when it boots?

messages.author: Matlavox
messages.text: No, literally the only LED behavior is two feint flashes when the RST button is pressed. There is no bootup green light. And I don't see any sort of hearbeat light while it's on
messages.content_html: No, literally the only LED behavior is two feint flashes when the RST button is pressed. There is no bootup green light. And I don't see any sort of hearbeat light while it's on
messages.reply_html:
Christopher A. Wichura
All I see is it explicitly turning the LEDs off when shutting down. Does it not turn the green LED on when it boots?

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: Also, as an FYI, 1.14.1 also has the nRF power management enhancements, so the system will NOT boot if battery voltage is less than 3.3v. Just in case a customer calls because the voltage has dropped too low.
messages.content_html: Also, as an FYI, 1.14.1 also has the nRF power management enhancements, so the system will NOT boot if battery voltage is less than 3.3v. Just in case a customer calls because the voltage has dropped too low.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Matlavox
messages.reply_html:
Christopher A. Wichura
erm, that's not in mainline. sorry. that pull is one someone submitted. mainline still appears to want to turn the white LED on at boot.

messages.author: Christopher A. Wichura
messages.text: There is a new pull request to fix LEDs on the SolarPRO after the changes in 1.14.1: https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/2157
messages.content_html: There is a new pull request to fix LEDs on the SolarPRO after the changes in 1.14.1: https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/pull/2157
embeds_html:
fix(sensecap_solar): correct TX LED after remap & power-button pin ...
Updates the SenseCap Solar build flag P_LORA_TX_LED from 11 to 12 so LoRa TX activity is driven on the blue LED again, consistent with variants/sensecap_solar/variant.h (LED_BLUE / PIN_LED on pin 1...
